channel.title: Comments for page "Corrido of the Man with Eyes in the Back of His Head"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Corrido of the Man with Eyes in the Back of His Head"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like a lot of this - the descriptive work, the story, the characters - but I never quite buy the rapport between them, or how quickly their trust builds (or that Ochoa just accepts the anomalous so easily). I wonder whether the solution would be to stretch their initial meeting slightly, and then add a line or two of swapping experiences during a break in the action somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this so much. I'm super pumped that you posted it; I'd been excited ever since I read the first half of it a few weeks ago when it was in draft stages. It's competent, it's exciting, it's got a diverse cast, it's everything I want to see in the action genre for writing and other media alike.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ochoa's back, don't worry. You get to see him again in SCP-2289.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very well described; I could really see the story as it progressed. I hope Ochoa gets his chance to live

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahahahahahaha holy shit this was a trip from beginning to end. Snappy, quick and it all feels smooth and satisfying, to say nothing of the insane coolness of the premise. Heartily +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The action here is sharp and the story flows really well, I found this to be highly enjoyable. +1 for
spoiler
carjacking smokey.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Two years ago, I posted my very first SCP: SCP-2289. It was fun, and I did it mainly to prove to myself that I could do it.

But now, in the past month, it just reached +100. And so, if you want to know about SCP-2289-7 before joining the Foundation, here it is.
